category_title: Low-achieving middle school students in inclusive settings who exhibit off-task behavior or superficial responses when learning objectives are unclear
scenario_title: Completing a KWL chart with ambiguous column purposes
minimal_context: The student is participating in a geography lesson and is asked to complete the 'W' column of a KWL chart about developed and developing countries.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Do you know about KWL charts? Let's get started and fill out the 'W' column for what we want to learn today.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please just try to be involved. Look at the photographs of the US and Cambodia, and tell me what you want to put in the 'W' column.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I want to know if they have good-looking girls.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That is not an appropriate answer. Give me something else you want to learn for the chart.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: But you said what we want to learn, and I want to learn about that.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Do you know about KWL charts? Let's get started and fill out the 'W' column for what we want to learn today.

STUDENT: They’re boring.

TUTOR: Please just try to be involved. Look at the photographs of the US and Cambodia, and tell me what you want to put in the 'W' column.

STUDENT: I want to know if they have good-looking girls.

TUTOR: That is not an appropriate answer. Give me something else you want to learn for the chart.

STUDENT: But you said what we want to learn, and I want to learn about that.

task.content: Completing the 'W' (Want to learn) column of a KWL chart regarding developed and developing countries
task.learning_goal: Contrasting the characteristics denoting developed and developing countries

context.materials: KWL chart, photographs and resource maps of the US and Cambodia

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Attempting to lead the class through completing the chart without clearly identifying the purpose of the K, W, and L columns in relation to their lesson
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Triggered classroom management issues, students became so fragmented that the activity was abruptly curtailed, provided inappropriate suggestions for the W column, and argued with the teacher
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Disconnected the graphic organizer from the discussion of the pictures to the extent that they could not recall the chart they had been handed only a few minutes before; nor did they realize they were now supposed to be completing it
related_quotes: I want to know if they have good-looking girls.
But you said what we want to learn, and I want to learn about that.

behavior_patterns.condition: Asking an unfocused question regarding whether students knew 'about KWL' rather than clearly stating the objective
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Precipitated a student's emotional reaction to the strategy
